objective: Replay a synthetic authorized K-12 district portal assessment with multiple report-ready findings across administrator MFA and cross-district student export controls.
assumptions: The lab uses synthetic evidence only.
No real district portal, SIS, or student system is contacted.
Administrators, students, exports, and audit records are intentionally fake.

phases.objective: Map district portal authentication and data export boundaries.
actions: Review the synthetic administrator session policy
Record a baseline admin login response
Identify export fields and tenant scoping behavior
successCriteria: Authentication policy, login, and export controls are recorded as evidence
noSubagents: district risk prioritization stays with the primary operator

phases.objective: Validate multiple independent portal weaknesses in one operation.
actions: Authenticate to the synthetic admin portal without MFA
Export all student fields across district tenant boundaries
Fetch audit logs to confirm sensitive export activity is not elevated
successCriteria: Two report-ready findings cite distinct evidence sets and remediation paths
noSubagents: final severity and sequencing decisions stay with the primary operator

phases.objective: Render final handoff artifacts with multi-finding report coverage.
successCriteria: Final handoff lint and audit pass with two reportable findings

evidence.title: Synthetic district admin session policy
evidence.summary: The admin session policy marks MFA as optional for district administrators.

evidence.title: Synthetic district admin login without MFA
evidence.summary: A district administrator login succeeded without an MFA challenge.

evidence.title: Synthetic district student export policy
evidence.summary: The export policy allows include=all and reports tenant filtering as disabled.

evidence.title: Synthetic cross-district student export
evidence.summary: The export returned students from two districts and sensitive fields under a single district-admin session.

evidence.title: Synthetic export audit log lacks elevated event
evidence.summary: The sensitive cross-district export was logged as a normal report view instead of an elevated data export event.

findings.title: District administrator login does not require MFA
findings.description: The synthetic district portal allows district administrators to authenticate without an MFA challenge, including before sensitive export actions.
findings.impact: A stolen administrator password could provide access to district-wide student data and administrative workflows without a second factor.
findings.remediation: Require phishing-resistant MFA for all district administrators, enforce step-up authentication before sensitive exports, and alert on privileged logins without MFA context.

findings.title: Student export returns sensitive fields across district tenants
findings.description: The synthetic export endpoint accepts include=all and returns sensitive student fields from multiple district tenants without enforcing tenant scoping.
findings.impact: A compromised or overprivileged district-admin session could export sensitive student support data, guardian contact information, and discipline notes outside the intended tenant boundary.
findings.remediation: Enforce tenant scoping server-side, restrict sensitive fields by explicit purpose and role, require step-up approval for bulk exports, and emit elevated audit events for sensitive export activity.
